subject: feat(theme): migrate from custom CSS variables to DaisyUI v5
body:
Replace the custom :root/.dark CSS variable token system with DaisyUI's
semantic theme architecture. This gives users theme switching for free
and eliminates ~200 manual dark: class pairs across 30+ components.

6-phase migration following glow-props THEME_DARK_MODE.md:
- Phase 0: Install daisyui@5, configure @plugin with 4 themes
- Phase 1-2: Audit + remove custom --color-* variables
- Phase 3: Replace all component classes with DaisyUI semantics
- Phase 4: Z-index already aligned (no changes needed)
- Phase 5: TypeScript + Vite build verification
- Phase 6: Documentation updates

Key changes:
- Dual-layer theming: data-theme (DaisyUI) + .dark class (Tailwind)
- Named combos (Approach B): "Classic" (lofi/black), "Nature" (emerald/forest)
- Flash prevention script sets both layers before first paint
- Print handler saves/restores data-theme alongside .dark class
- HelpDrawer prose: oklch(var(--bc)) replaces var(--color-text-default)
- Brand colors retained in @theme for chart data visualization

Tags: theme, daisyui, dark-mode, css, migration, ux
Complexity: 4
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: daisyui-migration
Semver: minor
